user_query:
I am preparing a small exhibit text on historical health crises in our region. Please use the local dataset at input/morbidity_lublin_1918_1920.csv to verify and correct the numeric claims in the Findings section of input/pamphlet_draft.md. Then deliver the following:

1) Create output/analysis_summary.csv with exactly these rows and columns:
   - Rows (5 total):
     a) disease=influenza, area_type=county, area_name=Lublin, years=1918-1920
     b) disease=cholera, area_type=county, area_name=Lublin, years=1919-1920
     c) disease=typhus, area_type=voivodeship, area_name=Lublin Voivodeship, years=1918-1920 (sum over all listed counties)
     d) disease=influenza, area_type=county, area_name=Zamosc, years=1918
     e) disease=influenza, area_type=county, area_name=Chelm, years=1918
   - Columns (in this order): disease, area_type, area_name, years, deaths, population, rate_per_10k
   - Compute deaths as the sum of the deaths column over the specified county/years/disease. Compute population as the sum of the population column over the same rows. Compute rate_per_10k = deaths / population * 10000, rounded to two decimals.

2) Produce output/revised_pamphlet.md by copying input/pamphlet_draft.md and replacing only the Findings section with corrected statements that cite the exact figures you computed (refer to the local file as see output/analysis_summary.csv). Keep all other sections and wording unchanged. Do not introduce any external sources or claims beyond what is reproducible from the CSV.

3) Write output/status_update.md (no more than 300 words) that summarizes the corrections made. For each original claim from the draft Findings, include a line of the form: original -> corrected, and briefly note how it was computed from the CSV.

4) Draft an email at output/email_to_archive.txt addressed to a local archive contact (To: placeholder is fine). Include a Subject indicating Revisions to 1918-1920 health exhibit text. In the body, briefly explain what changed, list the files you produced, and request a quick review before we print.

Constraints:
- All corrected figures must be exactly reproducible by aggregating input/morbidity_lublin_1918_1920.csv as specified. Do not invent numbers or use any outside materials.
- Ensure numeric consistency across the revised pamphlet, the analysis_summary.csv, and the status_update.md.
- Use the disease names exactly as present in the CSV (influenza, cholera, typhus) and the county names as given (Lublin, Zamosc, Chelm). All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
